# xui_shadowsocks_client (Resource)

Shadowsocks user (client) on an existing 3x-ui inbound. Managed via `/panel/api/clients/*` (add, get, update, del).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "xui_inbound" "example" {
  protocol = "shadowsocks"
  remark   = "example-shadowsocks"
  port     = 8388
  settings = jsonencode({
    method   = "aes-256-gcm"
    password = "inbound-shared-secret"
    network  = "tcp,udp"
    clients  = []
  })
  stream_settings = jsonencode({
    network  = "tcp"
    security = "none"
    tcpSettings = {
      acceptProxyProtocol = false
      header              = { type = "none" }
    }
  })
  sniffing = "{}"
}

resource "xui_shadowsocks_client" "example" {
  inbound_id = xui_inbound.example.id
  email      = "ss-client@example.com"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `email` (String) Unique client email / label in the panel.
- `inbound_id` (Number) Panel inbound id (number from URL / API).

### Optional

- `comment` (String)
- `enable` (Boolean)
- `expiry_time` (Number) Expiry in milliseconds since Unix epoch (0 = never).
- `limit_ip` (Number)
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Shadowsocks client password. If omitted, the panel generates one on create.
- `reset` (Number)
- `sub_id` (String)
- `tg_id` (Number)
- `total_gb` (Number) Traffic limit in **bytes** (panel field `totalGB`; 0 = unlimited).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Client password from the panel (server-generated unless `password` is set).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import xui_shadowsocks_client.example 1:ss-client@example.com
```
